summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2023-009, a variance request for a shed placed approximately five feet from the right-of-way instead of the previously approved 18-foot setback. The discrepancy was attributed to miscommunication between the homeowner and an Amish contractor. After discussion about precedent-setting concerns, the board approved the variance. Staff also announced a full BZA board with alternates filled and the hiring of a second zoning inspector.
decisions: Approved video minutes from previous meeting (Holmes yes, Segal yes, Theodore yes, Pruitt abstained)
Approved variance BZA 2023-009 allowing shed to remain approximately five feet from the right-of-way (Holmes yes, Segal abstained, Theodore yes, Pruitt yes)
action_items: New zoning inspector to begin employment on July 31st pending completion of pre-employment screening
New zoning inspector to begin training and attend future BZA meetings
key_amounts: Current actual setback: approximately 5 feet from right-of-way
notable_topics: Variance request BZA 2023-009 for shed placement closer than previously approved
Miscommunication between homeowner and Amish contractor regarding setback measurement
Concerns about setting precedent for future variance violations
Enforcement of BZA decisions when structures are already built
Formal complaint filed by concerned citizen regarding the shed placement
Announcement of full BZA board with alternates filled
attendees: Derek (BZA member, referenced by first name)
notes: The applicant, Mr. Smith, noted he was still displaced from his home due to a tornado. Board member Segal abstained from the final vote on the variance, and Pruitt abstained from approving prior meeting minutes. Significant concern was expressed by multiple board members about the precedent of approving a variance for a structure already built in violation of a prior variance approval. The person who filed the formal complaint was not present at the hearing. The meeting adjourned at 6:58 PM.
title: BZA Meeting 7-17-2023
